description_long: This object is a large, roughly rectangular stone block with a partially cylindrical column segment attached to its front. The stone appears to be marble or a similar light-colored, veined stone, with a smooth, polished finish on the cylindrical section and a rougher, more weathered texture on the back and sides. The top of the block is irregular and broken, suggesting it is a fragment of a larger architectural element. The base is squared and flat, providing stability, while the cylindrical portion features a rounded base molding. There is a visible green marking or graffiti on one side, indicating possible modern handling or cataloging. The overall appearance suggests it is a remnant from a classical or historical structure, possibly used as a barrier or boundary marker in an urban or archaeological setting.
description: A large, broken marble block with a partial cylindrical column and a flat base, featuring both smooth and rough surfaces and a green marking on one side.
description_view_0: Front view shows the cylindrical column segment attached to the block, with a flat base and smooth, veined marble surface.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the depth of the block and the transition from the smooth column to the rougher, squared stone.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the rough, unfinished stone surface with no cylindrical features, emphasizing the block's irregular breakage.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the green marking and the profile of the column base, with a mix of smooth and rough textures.
attribute_location_context: fragment of a classical architectural barrier in an urban archaeological site
attribute_installation_method: set on a flat foundation or embedded in the ground
attribute_weathering_pattern: Some surface roughness and chipping, especially on the broken top and rear; minor discoloration and a modern marking on one side.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: A green painted marking is visible on one side; no engravings or inscriptions present.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: appears isolated but likely once formed part of a continuous architectural barrier or column row
